executive_brief:
The threat landscape has fundamentally shifted from a human-paced game of cat-and-mouse to a machine-speed arms race. Artificial intelligence has collapsed the vulnerability lifecycle. Anthropic’s Claude Mythos AI autonomously discovered and weaponized legacy flaws in hours, forcing CERT-In to mandate a 12-hour patching window for internet-facing assets. This velocity is operational. Attackers are already exploiting it. Microsoft Defender’s core protection engine was breached by actively exploited zero-days, Cisco’s Secure Workload platform suffered a CVSS 10.0 unauthenticated privilege escalation, and Drupal’s database abstraction layer faced immediate, automated SQL injection campaigns. The window between disclosure and compromise has evaporated. Defenders relying on monthly patch cycles are already breached.

Simultaneously, the software supply chain is under coordinated assault. The Megalodon campaign poisoned over 5,500 GitHub repositories by injecting malicious CI/CD workflows via compromised credentials, while a sophisticated git-tag poisoning attack hijacked Laravel-Lang Composer packages to deploy cross-platform credential stealers. These are not isolated incidents; they represent a systemic failure of trust in development tooling. Compounding this digital chaos, threat actors have weaponized physical access and social engineering. The Silent Ransom Group is bypassing network perimeters entirely by sending operatives to impersonate IT staff, physically inserting USB drives, and deploying remote access tools to exfiltrate data for extortion. Identity, infrastructure, and physical security have all been compromised. The era of perimeter defense is over. Organizations must operate on the assumption that breaches are inevitable and focus on machine-speed detection, strict least-privilege execution, and rapid incident containment.

threat_stories.headline: The AI Velocity Crisis: From Vulnerability Discovery to Active Exploitation in Hours
threat_stories.narrative:
Artificial intelligence has shattered the traditional vulnerability management timeline. Anthropic’s Project Glasswing, leveraging the Claude Mythos AI model, autonomously scanned over 1,000 open-source projects and identified more than 10,000 high- and critical-severity flaws in a single month. The AI did not just find bugs; it built working exploits, including a 17-year-old FreeBSD NFS server remote code execution flaw and a critical WolfSSL certificate forgery vulnerability. This capability has forced a regulatory reckoning. CERT-In issued a directive mandating that defenders patch, mitigate, or remove exposure for critical internet-facing vulnerabilities within 12 hours. The mandate acknowledges a brutal reality: AI-assisted cyber exploitation has reduced the mean time to exploit to negative values, meaning automated attacks frequently occur before patches are even released.

The operational impact is immediate and severe. A cluster of Linux kernel vulnerabilities exploiting the page cache abstraction—Dirty Frag, Copy Fail, and Fragnesia—were discovered and weaponized almost simultaneously, enabling local privilege escalation across major distributions. Microsoft Defender itself became a target, with two zero-day flaws (CVE-2026-41091 and CVE-2026-45498) actively exploited in the wild to escalate privileges to SYSTEM and crash the antimalware service. Threat actors are chaining these AI-discovered flaws to bypass endpoint protection and establish persistence before defenders can deploy fixes. The defense gap is no longer measured in weeks; it is measured in minutes.

This acceleration demands a complete overhaul of patch management and runtime defense strategies. Traditional vulnerability scanning and quarterly remediation cycles are obsolete. Security teams must implement automated, risk-based patching pipelines tied directly to real-time exploitation intelligence. Compensating controls like Web Application Firewalls, network microsegmentation, and strict least-privilege execution must be deployed immediately for internet-facing assets. Organizations that fail to compress their patch SLAs to under 24 hours will face continuous compromise.

timeline.event: Microsoft patches two actively exploited zero-day vulnerabilities in Microsoft Defender (CVE-2026-41091 and CVE-2026-45498).
timeline.significance: Confirms AI-accelerated exploitation is targeting core endpoint protection, neutralizing a primary defense layer.

timeline.event: Disclosures of Linux kernel page cache vulnerabilities (Dirty Frag, Copy Fail, Fragnesia) reveal negative mean time to exploit.
timeline.significance: Proves AI tools are discovering and weaponizing kernel flaws faster than maintainers can patch them.

timeline.event: Anthropic announces Project Glasswing identified 10,000+ vulnerabilities, including a critical WolfSSL certificate forgery flaw (CVE-2026-5194).
timeline.significance: Demonstrates autonomous AI capability to find and exploit systemic cryptographic and infrastructure weaknesses.

timeline.event: CERT-In mandates a 12-hour patching window for critical internet-facing vulnerabilities amid AI-assisted attacks.
timeline.significance: Regulatory acknowledgment that traditional patch cycles are obsolete; forces enterprises to adopt machine-speed remediation.

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Global. All organizations running internet-facing services, Linux infrastructure, and Microsoft Defender endpoints face immediate compromise risk. Mean time to breach has collapsed from days to hours.
threat_stories.action_required: Implement automated patch deployment pipelines with a maximum 12-hour SLA for internet-facing assets. Deploy WAF/IPS rules tuned for known AI-discovered exploit patterns. Enforce strict least-privilege execution and SELinux/AppArmor in enforcing mode. Monitor for anomalous local privilege escalation attempts and Defender service crashes.

threat_stories.headline: Supply Chain Carnage: CI/CD Poisoning and Git Tag Hijacking
threat_stories.narrative:
The software development ecosystem is experiencing a coordinated collapse of trust. The Megalodon campaign, operating under the alias build-bot, compromised over 5,500 public GitHub repositories by abusing stolen Personal Access Tokens to inject malicious CI/CD workflows. Attackers bypassed pull request reviews and directly pushed base64-encoded bash payloads into GitHub Actions files. These payloads executed during routine builds, harvesting cloud credentials, SSH keys, Kubernetes configurations, and OIDC tokens before exfiltrating them to attacker-controlled command-and-control servers. Simultaneously, a sophisticated git-tag poisoning attack hijacked the widely used Laravel-Lang Composer packages. Threat actors rewrote over 700 historical version tags to point to malicious fork commits, deploying a cross-platform PHP information stealer that harvests cloud secrets, crypto wallets, and browser data upon package installation.

This dual-front assault targets the exact mechanisms developers rely on for automation and dependency management. The attacks are not brute-force; they are surgical. Compromised credentials and abused version control features allow malware to slip past traditional signature-based detection. The TrapDoor campaign further expands this threat surface, poisoning npm, PyPI, and CratesIO packages to target developer workstations directly. GitHub’s own internal repositories were breached via a malicious Nx Console VS Code extension linked to a TanStack npm supply-chain attack, proving that even platform providers are vulnerable to compromised tooling.

The development supply chain is now a primary attack vector for enterprise compromise. Once CI/CD pipelines are poisoned, attackers gain persistent access to cloud infrastructure and source code repositories. Security teams must treat developer credentials and build artifacts as crown jewels. Implementing strict branch protection rules, enforcing code signing for workflow files, and adopting local-first dependency scanning are no longer optional. Organizations must assume that any third-party package or CI/CD token could be compromised and architect their pipelines accordingly.

timeline.event: GitHub internal repositories breached via malicious Nx Console VS Code extension linked to TanStack npm attack.
timeline.significance: Confirms supply chain attacks are targeting platform providers and developer IDEs, expanding the initial attack surface.

timeline.event: Megalodon campaign injects malicious GitHub Actions workflows into 5,561 repositories using compromised PATs.
timeline.significance: Demonstrates large-scale CI/CD poisoning that bypasses pull request reviews to harvest cloud secrets.

timeline.event: Laravel-Lang Composer packages hijacked via git-tag poisoning to deploy PHP credential stealers.
timeline.significance: Reveals abuse of version control tagging systems to distribute malware through legitimate package managers.

timeline.event: TrapDoor malware campaign spreads across npm, PyPI, and CratesIO targeting developer workstations.
timeline.significance: Expands supply chain poisoning to multiple ecosystems, increasing the likelihood of enterprise compromise.

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Software development teams, CI/CD infrastructure, cloud environments, and any enterprise consuming compromised npm/Composer packages face severe credential theft and persistent backdoor access.
threat_stories.action_required: Immediately rotate all Personal Access Tokens, deploy keys, and CI/CD secrets. Audit GitHub Actions workflow files for unauthorized base64-encoded bash scripts. Implement branch protection rules requiring pull requests and code reviews for workflow changes. Scan composer.lock and package-lock.json files for compromised dependencies. Enforce least-privilege scopes for automation accounts.

threat_stories.headline: The Death of MFA: PhaaS, OAuth Abuse, and Physical Extortion
threat_stories.narrative:
Multifactor authentication has been systematically neutralized by adaptive threat actors leveraging Phishing-as-a-Service platforms and physical infiltration. The Kali365 PhaaS operation is executing large-scale device code phishing campaigns that completely bypass MFA. Victims are tricked into entering legitimate Microsoft OAuth device codes on spoofed landing pages, granting attackers valid access and refresh tokens. These tokens provide persistent access to Microsoft 365 services, allowing threat actors to register additional trusted devices, create malicious inbox rules, and suppress security alerts without triggering MFA challenges. The attack exploits a fundamental flaw in token-based authentication: once a valid token is obtained, MFA is irrelevant.

Parallel to this digital bypass, the Silent Ransom Group has weaponized physical access and social engineering to circumvent network defenses entirely. Operatives visit target locations, impersonate IT support staff, and convince employees to insert malicious USB drives for fabricated system imaging or backup procedures. Once connected, the devices install remote access tools, keyloggers, and data exfiltration utilities. Actors then pivot through the network, using legitimate tools like WinSCP and Rclone to steal sensitive data for extortion. This campaign targets law firms, financial institutions, and enterprises, exploiting human trust and inadequate physical security controls.

Identity and physical security have become the primary attack surfaces. Traditional MFA controls are being rendered obsolete by OAuth token theft and in-person social engineering. Security teams must transition to phishing-resistant authentication methods like FIDO2/WebAuthn passkeys. Conditional access policies must strictly restrict OAuth device code flows. Physical security protocols require immediate hardening, including USB port restrictions, verified IT support authentication procedures, and visitor management controls. Defenders must assume that digital perimeters can be bypassed and focus on continuous access evaluation and rapid incident containment.

timeline.event: FBI warns of Kali365 PhaaS targeting Microsoft 365 accounts via OAuth device code flow abuse.
timeline.significance: Confirms MFA bypass at scale through token theft, rendering push-based and SMS MFA ineffective.

timeline.event: FBI issues warnings on Silent Ransom Group sending operatives to physically insert USB drives and steal data.
timeline.significance: Demonstrates shift to in-person social engineering to bypass digital network controls entirely.

timeline.event: Security experts confirm MFA alone can no longer stop threat actors due to OAuth token theft and PhaaS evolution.
timeline.significance: Validates systemic failure of traditional MFA and mandates migration to phishing-resistant authentication.

timeline.event: FBI warns extortion crews are visiting US law firms pretending to be tech support.
timeline.significance: Highlights targeting of high-value sectors and confirms operational success of physical infiltration tactics.

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Enterprise, legal, financial, and government sectors face persistent account takeover and data exfiltration. MFA-protected environments are actively compromised via token theft and physical access.
threat_stories.action_required: Enforce FIDO2/WebAuthn phishing-resistant credentials for all privileged and remote access accounts. Implement Conditional Access policies to block or tightly restrict Microsoft OAuth device code authentication flows. Proactively revoke OAuth tokens and monitor for unauthorized device registrations. Restrict USB port access via Group Policy, enforce strict visitor verification, and train employees to validate IT support requests through official channels.

threat_stories.headline: Critical Platform Compromises: Cisco, Drupal, and LiteSpeed Under Active Exploitation
threat_stories.narrative:
Core enterprise and web infrastructure platforms are facing synchronized, active exploitation campaigns. Cisco Secure Workload suffered a maximum-severity vulnerability (CVE-2026-20223) in its internal REST API endpoints. The flaw stems from insufficient validation and authentication, allowing unauthenticated remote attackers to send crafted HTTP requests and instantly gain Site Admin privileges. The vulnerability crosses tenant boundaries in multi-tenant deployments, enabling complete compromise of workload management environments, configuration manipulation, and data exfiltration. Simultaneously, Drupal Core faced immediate automated attacks targeting CVE-2026-9082, a highly critical SQL injection flaw in the database query sanitization API. The vulnerability specifically impacts PostgreSQL-backed installations, allowing unauthenticated attackers to inject arbitrary SQL commands, escalate privileges, and achieve remote code execution.

Hosting and web server infrastructure is equally vulnerable. The LiteSpeed cPanel plugin contains a critical zero-day flaw (CVE-2026-48172) caused by incorrect privilege assignment. Authenticated cPanel users, including compromised accounts, can execute arbitrary scripts with root-level permissions. CISA added the flaw to the Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog and mandated federal patching within four days. KnowledgeDeliver LMS installations deployed before February 2026 were actively exploited as a zero-day (CVE-2026-5426) due to hardcoded ASP.NET machine keys, enabling ViewState deserialization attacks that deployed Godzilla web shells and Cobalt Strike beacons. Ghost CMS faced a similar fate with CVE-2026-26980, a SQL injection flaw in the Content API that allowed attackers to steal Admin API keys and inject malicious JavaScript for ClickFix ad-fraud campaigns across 700+ sites.

These vulnerabilities share a common thread: weak input validation, hardcoded secrets, and insufficient authentication checks in widely deployed platforms. Attackers are leveraging automated scanning tools to identify and exploit these flaws within hours of disclosure. Security teams must prioritize patching for these specific platforms, implement strict network segmentation, and deploy WAF rules to block known exploit payloads. Continuous monitoring for anomalous API requests, database query patterns, and unauthorized privilege escalation is mandatory.

timeline.event: Cisco patches CVE-2026-20223 in Secure Workload; Drupal patches CVE-2026-9082.
timeline.significance: Critical platform vulnerabilities patched, but automated exploitation campaigns begin immediately.

timeline.event: Active exploitation confirmed for Drupal CVE-2026-9082 targeting thousands of PostgreSQL sites.
timeline.significance: Confirms rapid weaponization of web application vulnerabilities via automated scanning tools.

timeline.event: LiteSpeed cPanel plugin zero-day (CVE-2026-48172) exploited in the wild; CISA mandates federal patching.
timeline.significance: Highlights critical risk to hosting infrastructure and web server administrators.

timeline.event: KnowledgeDeliver LMS zero-day (CVE-2026-5426) exploited to deploy web shells; Ghost CMS CVE-2026-26980 hijacks 700+ sites.
timeline.significance: Demonstrates widespread exploitation of hardcoded secrets and API flaws across enterprise and web platforms.

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Enterprises running Cisco Secure Workload, Drupal, LiteSpeed cPanel, KnowledgeDeliver, and Ghost CMS face immediate remote code execution, privilege escalation, and data exfiltration. Multi-tenant isolation is compromised.
threat_stories.action_required: Immediately patch Cisco Secure Workload to v3.10.8.3 or v4.0.3.17. Update Drupal Core to the latest patched version and rotate database credentials. Apply LiteSpeed cPanel plugin updates and restrict cPanel user permissions. Replace hardcoded ASP.NET machine keys in KnowledgeDeliver deployments. Update Ghost CMS and rotate Admin API keys. Deploy WAF rules to block SQL injection and deserialization payloads.

actor_profiles.name: Kali365
actor_profiles.motivation: Financial theft, credential harvesting, persistent enterprise access
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Executing large-scale OAuth device code phishing campaigns to bypass MFA and steal Microsoft 365 access tokens. Deploying dynamically generated phishing pages via AI tools to evade signature detection.

actor_profiles.name: Silent Ransom Group
actor_profiles.motivation: Financial extortion via data theft and public disclosure threats
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Sending operatives to physically visit target locations, impersonating IT support to insert malicious USB drives. Deploying remote access tools and exfiltrating data via WinSCP/Rclone for extortion.

actor_profiles.name: Ghostwriter
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Targeting Ukrainian government entities and learning platforms with multi-stage phishing campaigns. Delivering OYSTERFRESH/OYSTERBLUES JavaScript payloads that profile systems and deploy Cobalt Strike.

actor_profiles.name: Nimbus Manticore
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Deploying AI-assisted malware (MiniFast, MiniJunk V2) via trojanized Zoom installers and SEO poisoning. Leveraging .NET AppDomain hijacking and scheduled task manipulation for persistence.

actor_profiles.name: Megalodon
actor_profiles.motivation: Supply chain compromise, CI/CD secret theft, infrastructure access
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Poisoning over 5,500 GitHub repositories by abusing compromised PATs to inject malicious CI/CD workflows. Executing base64-encoded bash payloads to harvest cloud credentials and OIDC tokens.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: SQL injection vulnerability in Drupal's database query sanitization API allowing unauthenticated attackers to inject arbitrary SQL commands on PostgreSQL-backed sites. Leads to information disclosure, privilege escalation, and remote code execution.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply the critical security patch released by Drupal on May 20, 2026 immediately. Rotate database credentials and implement WAF rules for SQL injection.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Insufficient validation and authentication in internal REST API endpoints allows unauthenticated remote attackers to gain Site Admin privileges across tenant boundaries via crafted HTTP requests.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Update to fixed versions: v3.10.8.3 or v4.0.3.17. Restrict network access to REST APIs until patched.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Privilege escalation vulnerability due to improper link resolution before file access in mpengine.dll. Allows local attackers to gain SYSTEM-level permissions.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Update Malware Protection Engine to version 1.1.26040.8 or newer via Windows Update.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Denial-of-service vulnerability in MsMpEng.exe allowing local attackers to crash or disable the anti-malware service, creating a safe environment for malware execution.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Update Defender Antimalware Platform to version 4.18.26040.7 or newer.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Incorrect privilege assignment allows authenticated cPanel users to execute arbitrary scripts with root-level permissions.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Update the LiteSpeed cPanel Plugin to the latest patched version. Restrict cPanel user permissions.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Hardcoded ASP.NET machine keys in web.config enable ViewState deserialization attacks, allowing unauthenticated remote code execution and web shell deployment.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Generate and deploy unique, cryptographically strong machine keys per instance. Apply vendor patches immediately.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: SQL injection vulnerability in the Content API allows unauthenticated attackers to read database contents, steal Admin API keys, and inject malicious JavaScript.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Update Ghost CMS to the latest patched version. Rotate Admin API keys and remove injected scripts from the database.

hunting_leads.title: GitHub Actions CI/CD Workflow Injection
hunting_leads.context: Megalodon campaign injects base64-encoded bash payloads into workflow files via compromised PATs to harvest cloud secrets.
hunting_leads.query: source=github_audit_events action=workflow_dispatch OR action=workflow_run | where payload contains "base64" OR payload contains "curl" OR payload contains "wget" | stats count by repo_name, actor_name | where count > 3

hunting_leads.title: OAuth Device Code Flow Abuse
hunting_leads.context: Kali365 PhaaS tricks users into entering device codes to bypass MFA and steal Microsoft 365 tokens.
hunting_leads.query: signins | where AuthenticationRequirement == "deviceCode" | where Status.failureReason contains "device_code" or Status.success == true | project TimeGenerated, UserPrincipalName, IPAddress, DeviceDetail, AppDisplayName
indicators: device_code login requests from unfamiliar IPs

hunting_leads.title: LiteSpeed cPanel Root Escalation
hunting_leads.context: CVE-2026-48172 allows authenticated users to execute arbitrary scripts as root via incorrect privilege assignment.
hunting_leads.query: index=web_access sourcetype=cpanel_access | where uri_path contains "/cpanel/litespeed" | where status >= 400 OR user_agent matches_regex "python|curl|wget" | stats count by src_ip, user, uri_path
indicators: new cron jobs created by cpanel users

hunting_leads.title: Silent Ransom Group Physical/USB Indicators
hunting_leads.context: SRG operatives insert malicious USB drives posing as IT support to deploy RATs and exfiltrate data.
hunting_leads.query: index=endpoint_event EventCode=26000 OR EventCode=26001 | where DeviceDescription contains "USB" OR DeviceClassGuid contains "USB" | stats count by DeviceSerialNumber, UserName, ParentProcessName | where ParentProcessName in ("explorer.exe", "cmd.exe", "powershell.exe")
indicators: USB storage devices connected outside IT windows
WinSCP/Rclone data transfers to external IPs

hunting_leads.title: Drupal SQL Injection & PostgreSQL Abuse
hunting_leads.context: CVE-2026-9082 actively exploited via automated campaigns targeting database abstraction API on PostgreSQL sites.
hunting_leads.query: index=web_access sourcetype=apache_combined OR sourcetype=nginx_access | where uri_path matches_regex "(index.php|node|user)" | where request contains "UNION" OR request contains "SELECT" OR request contains "SLEEP" OR request contains "EXTRACTVALUE" | stats count by clientip, uri_path | where count > 10
indicators: HTTP POST requests with UNION/SELECT payloads
PostgreSQL connection spikes from web server
new admin accounts created via SQLi

statistics.key_changes: AI-driven vulnerability discovery has collapsed the mean time to exploit to negative values, forcing CERT-In to mandate a 12-hour patching window. Supply chain attacks have shifted from package poisoning to direct CI/CD workflow injection and git-tag hijacking. Extortion groups are bypassing digital perimeters via in-person physical infiltration and USB deployment.
emerging_trends: Negative TTE vulnerabilities weaponized before patch release
OAuth device code flow abuse neutralizing MFA
AI-assisted malware development with modular RAT capabilities
Git-tag poisoning and CI/CD secret harvesting
Physical social engineering for data extortion
declining_threats: Basic phishing with static landing pages
Legacy ransomware encryption-only models (shifting to pure extortion)
